automatedPageContext.renderedPage:
About permissions for GitHub Actions

You can use the REST API to set permissions for the organizations and repositories that are allowed to run GitHub Actions, and the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run. For more information, see Billing and usage.
automatedPageContext.title: REST API endpoints for GitHub Actions permissions

rest.screen_reader_text_checkmark_icon: Multiple permissions are required, or a different permission may be used. For more information about the permissions, see the documentation for this endpoint.

rest_reference.basic_auth: You must use Basic Authentication to use this endpoint. Use the application's client_id as the username and the client_secret as the password.

page.fullTitle: REST API endpoints for GitHub Actions permissions - GitHub Docs
page.introPlainText: Use the REST API to interact with permissions for GitHub Actions.
page.title: REST API endpoints for GitHub Actions permissions

childPages.title: Endpoints for GitHub App installation tokens
Endpoints for GitHub App user tokens

allowed_actions.description: The permissions policy that controls the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run.

enabled_repositories.description: The policy that controls the repositories in the organization that are allowed to run GitHub Actions.

selected_actions_url.description: The API URL to use to get or set the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run, when `allowed_actions` is set to `selected`.

selected_repositories_url.description: The API URL to use to get or set the selected repositories that are allowed to run GitHub Actions, when `enabled_repositories` is set to `selected`.

sha_pinning_required.description: Whether actions must be pinned to a full-length commit SHA.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for repositories and allowed actions and reusable workflows in an organization.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Get GitHub Actions permissions for an organization

bodyParameters.description: The policy that controls the repositories in the organization that are allowed to run GitHub Actions.
bodyParameters.name: enabled_repositories

bodyParameters.description: The permissions policy that controls the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run.
bodyParameters.name: allowed_actions

bodyParameters.description: Whether actions must be pinned to a full-length commit SHA.
bodyParameters.name: sha_pinning_required

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Sets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for repositories and allowed actions and reusable workflows in an organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization

days.description: The number of days artifacts and logs are retained

maximum_allowed_days.description: The maximum number of days that can be configured

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets artifact and log retention settings for an organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope or the "Actions policies" fine-grained permission to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Get artifact and log retention settings for an organization

bodyParameters.description: The number of days to retain artifacts and logs
bodyParameters.name: days

restOperations.descriptionHTML:

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope or the "Actions policies" fine-grained permission to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

restOperations.title: Set artifact and log retention settings for an organization

approval_policy.description: The policy that controls when fork PR workflows require approval from a maintainer.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the fork PR contributor approval policy for an organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope or the "Actions policies" fine-grained permission to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Get fork PR contributor approval permissions for an organization

bodyParameters.description: The policy that controls when fork PR workflows require approval from a maintainer.
bodyParameters.name: approval_policy

request.description: Set approval policy to first time contributors

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Sets the fork PR contributor approval policy for an organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

restOperations.title: Set fork PR contributor approval permissions for an organization

require_approval_for_fork_pr_workflows.description: Whether workflows triggered by pull requests from forks require approval from a repository administrator to run.

run_workflows_from_fork_pull_requests.description: Whether workflows triggered by pull requests from forks are allowed to run on private repositories.

send_secrets_and_variables.description: Whether to make secrets and variables available to workflows triggered by pull requests from forks.

send_write_tokens_to_workflows.description: Whether GitHub Actions can create pull requests or submit approving pull request reviews from a workflow triggered by a fork pull request.

restOperations.descriptionHTML: Gets the settings for whether workflows from fork pull requests can run on private repositories in an organization.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Get private repo fork PR workflow settings for an organization

bodyParameters.description: Whether workflows triggered by pull requests from forks are allowed to run on private repositories.
bodyParameters.name: run_workflows_from_fork_pull_requests

bodyParameters.description: Whether GitHub Actions can create pull requests or submit approving pull request reviews from a workflow triggered by a fork pull request.
bodyParameters.name: send_write_tokens_to_workflows

bodyParameters.description: Whether to make secrets and variables available to workflows triggered by pull requests from forks.
bodyParameters.name: send_secrets_and_variables

bodyParameters.description: Whether workflows triggered by pull requests from forks require approval from a repository administrator to run.
bodyParameters.name: require_approval_for_fork_pr_workflows

restOperations.descriptionHTML: Sets the settings for whether workflows from fork pull requests can run on private repositories in an organization.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

statusCodes.description: Forbidden - Fork PR workflow settings for private repositories are managed by the enterprise owner
Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

restOperations.title: Set private repo fork PR workflow settings for an organization

allow_auto_merge.description: Whether to allow Auto-merge to be used on pull requests.

allow_forking.description: Whether to allow forking this repo

allow_merge_commit.description: Whether to allow merge commits for pull requests.

allow_rebase_merge.description: Whether to allow rebase merges for pull requests.

allow_squash_merge.description: Whether to allow squash merges for pull requests.

allow_update_branch.description: Whether or not a pull request head branch that is behind its base branch can always be updated even if it is not required to be up to date before merging.

anonymous_access_enabled.description: Whether anonymous git access is enabled for this repository

code_search_index_status.description: The status of the code search index for this repository

default_branch.description: The default branch of the repository.

delete_branch_on_merge.description: Whether to delete head branches when pull requests are merged

disabled.description: Returns whether or not this repository disabled.

is_template.description: Whether this repository acts as a template that can be used to generate new repositories.

merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a merge commit message.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a merge commit title.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `MERGE_MESSAGE` - default to the classic title for a merge message (e.g., Merge pull request #123 from branch-name).

private.description: Whether the repository is private or public.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

squash_merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit message:

- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `COMMIT_MESSAGES` - default to the branch's commit messages.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

squash_merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit title:

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE` - default to the commit's title (if only one commit) or the pull request's title (when more than one commit).

visibility.description: The repository visibility: public, private, or internal.

web_commit_signoff_required.description: Whether to require contributors to sign off on web-based commits

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Lists the selected repositories that are enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. To use this endpoint, the organization permission policy for enabled_repositories must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."
parameters.name: per_page

parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."
parameters.name: page

restOperations.title: List selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization

bodyParameters.description: List of repository IDs to enable for GitHub Actions.
bodyParameters.name: selected_repository_ids

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Replaces the list of selected repositories that are enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. To use this endpoint, the organization permission policy for enabled_repositories must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Set selected repositories enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Adds a repository to the list of selected repositories that are enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. To use this endpoint, the organization permission policy for enabled_repositories must be must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization."

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the repository.
parameters.name: repository_id

restOperations.title: Enable a selected repository for GitHub Actions in an organization

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Removes a repository from the list of selected repositories that are enabled for GitHub Actions in an organization. To use this endpoint, the organization permission policy for enabled_repositories must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization."

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the repository.
parameters.name: repository_id

restOperations.title: Disable a selected repository for GitHub Actions in an organization

github_owned_allowed.description: Whether GitHub-owned actions are allowed. For example, this includes the actions in the `actions` organization.

patterns_allowed.description:
Specifies a list of string-matching patterns to allow specific action(s) and reusable workflow(s). Wildcards, tags, and SHAs are allowed. For example, `monalisa/octocat@*`, `monalisa/octocat@v2`, `monalisa/*`.

> [!NOTE]
> The `patterns_allowed` setting only applies to public repositories.

verified_allowed.description: Whether actions from GitHub Marketplace verified creators are allowed. Set to `true` to allow all actions by GitHub Marketplace verified creators.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the selected actions and reusable workflows that are allowed in an organization. To use this endpoint, the organization permission policy for allowed_actions must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization."

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Get allowed actions and reusable workflows for an organization

bodyParameters.description: Whether GitHub-owned actions are allowed. For example, this includes the actions in the actions organization.
bodyParameters.name: github_owned_allowed

bodyParameters.description: Whether actions from GitHub Marketplace verified creators are allowed. Set to true to allow all actions by GitHub Marketplace verified creators.
bodyParameters.name: verified_allowed

bodyParameters.description:
Specifies a list of string-matching patterns to allow specific action(s) and reusable workflow(s). Wildcards, tags, and SHAs are allowed. For example, monalisa/octocat@*, monalisa/octocat@v2, monalisa/*.

Note

The patterns_allowed setting only applies to public repositories.
bodyParameters.name: patterns_allowed

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Sets the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed in an organization. To use this endpoint, the organization permission policy for allowed_actions must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for an organization."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Set allowed actions and reusable workflows for an organization

enabled_repositories.description: The policy that controls whether self-hosted runners can be used by repositories in the organization

selected_repositories_url.description: The URL to the endpoint for managing selected repositories for self-hosted runners in the organization

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the settings for self-hosted runners for an organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope or the "Actions policies" fine-grained permission to use this endpoint.

parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Get self-hosted runners settings for an organization

bodyParameters.description: The policy that controls whether self-hosted runners can be used in the organization

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

allow_rebase_merge.description: Whether to allow rebase merges for pull requests.

allow_squash_merge.description: Whether to allow squash merges for pull requests.

allow_update_branch.description: Whether or not a pull request head branch that is behind its base branch can always be updated even if it is not required to be up to date before merging.

anonymous_access_enabled.description: Whether anonymous git access is enabled for this repository

code_search_index_status.description: The status of the code search index for this repository

default_branch.description: The default branch of the repository.

delete_branch_on_merge.description: Whether to delete head branches when pull requests are merged

disabled.description: Returns whether or not this repository disabled.

is_template.description: Whether this repository acts as a template that can be used to generate new repositories.

merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a merge commit message.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a merge commit title.

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `MERGE_MESSAGE` - default to the classic title for a merge message (e.g., Merge pull request #123 from branch-name).

private.description: Whether the repository is private or public.

pull_request_creation_policy.description: The policy controlling who can create pull requests: all or collaborators_only.

size.description: The size of the repository, in kilobytes. Size is calculated hourly. When a repository is initially created, the size is 0.

squash_merge_commit_message.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit message:

- `PR_BODY` - default to the pull request's body.
- `COMMIT_MESSAGES` - default to the branch's commit messages.
- `BLANK` - default to a blank commit message.

squash_merge_commit_title.description:
The default value for a squash merge commit title:

- `PR_TITLE` - default to the pull request's title.
- `COMMIT_OR_PR_TITLE` - default to the commit's title (if only one commit) or the pull request's title (when more than one commit).

visibility.description: The repository visibility: public, private, or internal.

response.description: Give read-only permission, and allow approving PRs.

can_approve_pull_request_reviews.description: Whether GitHub Actions can approve pull requests. Enabling this can be a security risk.

default_workflow_permissions.description: The default workflow permissions granted to the GITHUB_TOKEN when running workflows.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the default workflow permissions granted to the GITHUB_TOKEN when running workflows in an organization, as well as whether GitHub Actions can submit approving pull request reviews. For more information, see "Setting the permissions of the GITHUB_TOKEN for your organization."

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Get default workflow permissions for an organization

bodyParameters.description: The default workflow permissions granted to the GITHUB_TOKEN when running workflows.
bodyParameters.name: default_workflow_permissions

bodyParameters.description: Whether GitHub Actions can approve pull requests. Enabling this can be a security risk.
bodyParameters.name: can_approve_pull_request_reviews

request.description: Give read-only permission, and allow approving PRs.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Sets the default workflow permissions granted to the GITHUB_TOKEN when running workflows in an organization, and sets if GitHub Actions can submit approving pull request reviews. For more information, see "Setting the permissions of the GITHUB_TOKEN for your organization."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the admin:org scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: org

restOperations.title: Set default workflow permissions for an organization

allowed_actions.description: The permissions policy that controls the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run.

enabled.description: Whether GitHub Actions is enabled on the repository.

selected_actions_url.description: The API URL to use to get or set the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run, when `allowed_actions` is set to `selected`.

sha_pinning_required.description: Whether actions must be pinned to a full-length commit SHA.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for a repository, including whether GitHub Actions is enabled and the actions and reusable workflows allowed to run in the repository.

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: repo

restOperations.title: Get GitHub Actions permissions for a repository

bodyParameters.description: Whether GitHub Actions is enabled on the repository.
bodyParameters.name: enabled

bodyParameters.description: The permissions policy that controls the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed to run.
bodyParameters.name: allowed_actions

bodyParameters.description: Whether actions must be pinned to a full-length commit SHA.
bodyParameters.name: sha_pinning_required

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Sets the GitHub Actions permissions policy for enabling GitHub Actions and allowed actions and reusable workflows in the repository.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: repo

restOperations.title: Set GitHub Actions permissions for a repository

access_level.description:
Defines the level of access that workflows outside of the repository have to actions and reusable workflows within the
repository.

`none` means the access is only possible from workflows in this repository. `user` level access allows sharing across user owned private repositories only. `organization` level access allows sharing across the organization.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the level of access that workflows outside of the repository have to actions and reusable workflows in the repository. This endpoint only applies to private repositories. For more information, see "Allowing access to components in a private repository."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: repo

restOperations.title: Get the level of access for workflows outside of the repository

bodyParameters.description:
Defines the level of access that workflows outside of the repository have to actions and reusable workflows within the repository.

none means the access is only possible from workflows in this repository. user level access allows sharing across user owned private repositories only. organization level access allows sharing across the organization.
bodyParameters.name: access_level

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Sets the level of access that workflows outside of the repository have to actions and reusable workflows in the repository. This endpoint only applies to private repositories. For more information, see "Allowing access to components in a private repository".

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: repo

require_approval_for_fork_pr_workflows.description: Whether workflows triggered by pull requests from forks require approval from a repository administrator to run.

send_secrets_and_variables.description: Whether to make secrets and variables available to workflows triggered by pull requests from forks.

send_write_tokens_to_workflows.description: Whether GitHub Actions can create pull requests or submit approving pull request reviews from a workflow triggered by a fork pull request.

github_owned_allowed.description: Whether GitHub-owned actions are allowed. For example, this includes the actions in the `actions` organization.

patterns_allowed.description:
Specifies a list of string-matching patterns to allow specific action(s) and reusable workflow(s). Wildcards, tags, and SHAs are allowed. For example, `monalisa/octocat@*`, `monalisa/octocat@v2`, `monalisa/*`.

> [!NOTE]
> The `patterns_allowed` setting only applies to public repositories.

verified_allowed.description: Whether actions from GitHub Marketplace verified creators are allowed. Set to `true` to allow all actions by GitHub Marketplace verified creators.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the settings for selected actions and reusable workflows that are allowed in a repository. To use this endpoint, the repository policy for allowed_actions must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for a repository."

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: repo

restOperations.title: Get allowed actions and reusable workflows for a repository

bodyParameters.description: Whether GitHub-owned actions are allowed. For example, this includes the actions in the actions organization.
bodyParameters.name: github_owned_allowed

bodyParameters.description: Whether actions from GitHub Marketplace verified creators are allowed. Set to true to allow all actions by GitHub Marketplace verified creators.
bodyParameters.name: verified_allowed

bodyParameters.description:
Specifies a list of string-matching patterns to allow specific action(s) and reusable workflow(s). Wildcards, tags, and SHAs are allowed. For example, monalisa/octocat@*, monalisa/octocat@v2, monalisa/*.

Note

The patterns_allowed setting only applies to public repositories.
bodyParameters.name: patterns_allowed

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Sets the actions and reusable workflows that are allowed in a repository. To use this endpoint, the repository permission policy for allowed_actions must be configured to selected. For more information, see "Set GitHub Actions permissions for a repository."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: owner

parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.
parameters.name: repo

restOperations.title: Set allowed actions and reusable workflows for a repository

response.description: Give read-only permission, and allow approving PRs.

can_approve_pull_request_reviews.description: Whether GitHub Actions can approve pull requests. Enabling this can be a security risk.

default_workflow_permissions.description: The default workflow permissions granted to the GITHUB_TOKEN when running workflows.

restOperations.descriptionHTML:
Gets the default workflow permissions granted to the GITHUB_TOKEN when running workflows in a repository, as well as if GitHub Actions can submit approving pull request reviews. For more information, see "Setting the permissions of the GITHUB_TOKEN for your repository."

OAuth tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.
